mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deepening labor market weakness during a recession, which reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, driving investors toward safe-haven Treasuries and pushing the 10-year yield lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If over any 2-year rolling window, the rank information coefficient between UNRATE level and 20-day forward yield change is greater than 0.00 (positive) or the mean yield change when UNRATE > 5% is not significantly negative (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200,000 intensifies recession fears and triggers a flight-to-quality bid for Treasuries, causing the 10-year yield to fall over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after a trigger event, the 20-day forward yield change is positive in more than 40% of cases, or the average decline is less than 10 basis points, over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and increases in the unemployment rate indicate simultaneous supply-side contraction and labor market distress, amplifying recession signals and intensifying demand for safe assets, which depresses the 10-year yield.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline and UNRATE month-over-month increase
failure_condition: If the joint signal (both conditions met) does not produce an average 20-day yield decline greater than 15 basis points with at least 60% hit rate over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or near the zero lower bound (<=0.5%), additional monetary easing through quantitative easing purchases of long-term securities compresses term premiums directly and signals prolonged accommodation, pushing the 10-year yield lower.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with quantitative easing
failure_condition: If within 20 trading days of FEDFUNDS dropping below 0.5%, the 10-year yield rises by more than 20 basis points more than 30% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 800 thousand units signals a collapse in residential investment, one of the most interest-sensitive sectors, reinforcing deflationary pressures and a depressed economic outlook, which lowers the 10-year yield within a short horizon.
failure_condition: If within 5 trading days after a release showing HOUST below 800, the yield does not decline in at least 65% of cases over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Falling consumer price inflation reduces the inflation premium embedded in nominal Treasury yields, and as real yields adjust downward in a low-inflation environment, the 10-year yield drifts lower over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If the rolling 2-year correlation between monthly CPIAUCSL changes and 60-day forward yield changes is positive and statistically significant (|t|>2) over any window during the walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP coupled with a rise in the unemployment rate confirms a deep recession with negative output gap, reducing expected future short-term rates and leading to a sustained decline in the 10-year yield over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter decline and UNRATE quarter-over-quarter increase
failure_condition: If after a joint signal, the 60-day forward yield change is positive in more than 35% of cases over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real retail sales exceeding 5% signals an abrupt collapse in consumer spending, the largest component of aggregate demand, prompting a sharp repricing of growth expectations and a short-term flight-to-quality that lowers the 10-year yield.
failure_condition: If within 5 trading days after the trigger, the 10-year yield does not decline by at least 5 basis points in more than 50% of occurrences over a 3-year walk-forward period.
